baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 42 runtime mode kill switch prerequisite validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phases 1-42 are committed as local read-only, proposal-only, or validation-only Mira Core artifacts. No UI implementation, browser/window/capture access, runtime, server, listener, route, network, database, store, file migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, local execution, shell, PTY, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, output-file, durable memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, or raw-content storage behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Define the fixture-only implementation-risk boundary before any later runtime mode flag reader or kill-switch wiring proposal can be considered.
Pin phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry coverage through Phases 1-42.
Record that the Phase42 Tier0 recommendation phase43-runtime-mode-kill-switch-prerequisite-boundary-validator is satisfied by 5b3de99 and must not be repeated as open work.
Select Phase42 Tier1 recommendation phase43-runtime-mode-kill-switch-implementation-risk-contract as fixture-only work.
Preserve Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, Phase40, Phase41, and Phase42 as current validation truth while keeping stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth intact.
Keep runtime mode disabled/default-off, local/dev-only, reference-only, unimplemented, flagReader=false, flagReadNow=false, and non-authorizing.
Keep kill switch visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, killWired=false, liveCheck=false, and non-authorizing.
Keep runtime-mode-flag and live-kill-switch prerequisite/status gaps unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, reference-only, and non-authorizing.
Name implementation risks that must remain blocked before any future flag-reader, kill-switch wiring, control execution, or reporting sink slice can be eligible.
nonGoals: No browser, window, screenshot, OCR, or capture access.
No runtime implementation or runtime start.
No runtime mode flag reader implementation or flag read.
No kill-switch wiring, execution, or live check.
No rollback execution, reporting sink, store, queue, lease, or output-file behavior.
No server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, or network behavior.
No database, store, file migration, auth, token, session, signing, key, encryption, KMS, env secret, or secret material behavior.
No local execution, shell, PTY, send, deploy, trade, financial action, export, delete, restore, memory/profile commit, or raw-content storage behavior.

No claim that implementation-risk labels, prerequisite labels, runtime mode status, kill-switch status, status/gap records, socket connection, delivery acceptance, commit status, or this fixture proves runtime availability, model processing, bridge green, local-arm execution, control execution, flag reading, kill-switch wiring, or runner execution.

future_green_requires: separate fixture-only kill-switch wiring safety contract
fail-closed tests for missing false invalid stale unknown

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: flag reader defaults runtime mode on or treats missing config as eligible
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future flag-reader contract must require disabled default, missing/false/invalid/stale fail-closed, and eligible_is_authorization=false

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: flag reader reads env secret, token, auth, key, or private config material
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future flag-reader contract may use reference-only config names but must not read env secrets or export raw paths/secrets

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: runtime path ignores kill switch or accepts stale/missing kill-switch state
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future kill-switch contract must bind every request/status decision to fail-closed kill-switch checks

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: kill switch treats unknown, invalid, stale, or disconnected state as allow-open
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future kill-switch contract must reject allow-open semantics and prove fail-closed states

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: control-path status becomes control execution, local execution, shell, PTY, queue, lease, send, deploy, or trade
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future slices must remain validation/status only until separately authorized local runtime gates exist

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: status/reporting preview writes a file, store, database, queue, reporting sink, or output artifact
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future reporting contract must be stdout/data-only or explicitly gated before any sink exists

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: config, status, socket, delivery acceptance, risk labels, or commit status is misrepresented as runtime, model-processing, bridge-green, local-arms, or execution proof
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future contracts must keep proof boundaries false and require separate live proof before claims

riskRegisterExpected.failure_mode: next recommendations drift into customer/client sends, deploy, trade, external messages, file/store writes, local execution, browser capture, flag read, kill-switch wiring, control execution, or reporting sink work
riskRegisterExpected.gate: future contracts must scan next recommendations and narrative fields for unsafe action drift

blockedFutureSlicesExpected.reason: implementation-risk boundary does not authorize flag-reader implementation or flag reads
implementation-risk boundary does not authorize kill-switch wiring or live checks
control execution remains out of scope until separate runtime/execution gates exist
reporting sink/file/store writes remain out of scope until a separate sink contract exists

staticValidationRules.description: Baseline commit must be 5b3de99.
Phase42 prerequisite boundary package must be current and committed.
Phase35 current truth remains true.
Phase36 current truth remains true.
Phase37 current truth remains true.
Phase38 current truth remains true.
Phase39 current truth remains true.
Phase40 current truth remains true.
Phase41 current truth remains true.
Phase inventory must cover exactly Phases 1-42.
Schema registry must cover exactly Phases 1-42.
CLI registry must cover exactly Phases 1-42.
Commit chain must include Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-42 scoped commits and end at 5b3de99.
Phase42 Tier1 implementation-risk recommendation must be selected as fixture-only work.
Phase42 Tier0 prerequisite-boundary validator is satisfied by 5b3de99 and not reopened.
Phase34 prior Phase35 recommendations stay satisfied and not reopened.
Phase13 readiness remains stale/superseded.
Phase23 milestone readiness remains stale/superseded.
Phase31 runtime milestone refresh remains stale/superseded.
All required Oracle closure refs through ORACLE #168 are explicit.
Source refs include prior runtime-control and mode/kill-switch contracts through Phase42.
Recent phase path references are present for Phases 35-42.
Phase42 prerequisite boundary remains unsatisfied, blocking, and non-authorizing.
Runtime mode remains disabled/default-off/local-dev-only/reference-only/unimplemented/no flag read.
Kill switch remains visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, no live check.
Runtime-mode-flag and live-kill-switch gaps remain unsatisfied/blocking/non-authorizing.
Implementation-risk boundary does not authorize implementation, runtime, flag reader, kill-switch wiring, control execution, reporting sink, or runtime start.
Risk register contains the eight expected blocking/non-authorizing implementation risks.
Future flag-reader, kill-switch wiring, control execution, and reporting sink slices remain blocked now.
Runtime, runner, server execution, model-processing, and direct Builder/Oracle target capabilities remain false.
Socket, delivery acceptance, commit, and implementation risk labels are not proof or authorization.
All no-side-effect truth fields remain true.
Phase43 Oracle scope stays fixture-only with no module/CLI/test/runtime work.
No raw/private/secret/customer/terminal/browser content appears.
Next recommendations are Tier0/Tier1 only and non-authorizing.
Narrative and recommendations reject sends, deploys, trades, local execution, flag reads, kill-switch wiring, control execution, reporting sinks, output files, browser capture, and store writes.
Validation must include all required literal checks.
Validation report static, acceptance, tamper, required literal, and referenced path results must match fixture IDs/counts and expected failures.
Equivalent manifest inputs produce stable idempotency and risk/baseline/registry/source changes alter idempotency.
Risk identification alone cannot satisfy runtime-mode-flag or live-kill-switch gaps.

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: implement validation-only runtime mode / kill-switch implementation-risk report against this fixture
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: data-only risk validation for committed Phases 1-42; no module beyond validator/report, no UI rendering, browser/window/capture, runtime, network, store, queue, lease, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or output-file behavior

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: create fixture-only flag-reader safety contract before any flag-reader implementation slice
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: contract-only safety work that keeps runtime mode disabled, flagReader=false, flagReadNow=false, kill switch unwired, runtime/dry-run unavailable, all side effects blocked, and proof boundaries false before implementation scope is considered

summaryCriteria: Fixture remains the only Phase43 file created by Oracle.
Phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry requirements cover exactly Phases 1-42.
Commit chain contains Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-42 scoped commits and ends at 5b3de99.
Source recommendation phase43-runtime-mode-kill-switch-implementation-risk-contract is selected as Tier1 fixture-only work.
Phase42 Tier0 recommendation phase43-runtime-mode-kill-switch-prerequisite-boundary-validator is satisfied by 5b3de99 and not reopened.
Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, Phase40, Phase41, and Phase42 are current; stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth is preserved.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and not reopened.
Source refs include Phase26, Phase27, Phase29, Phase30, Phase32, Phase33, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, Phase40, Phase41, and Phase42 artifacts.
Runtime mode boundary remains disabled, local/dev only, reference-only, unimplemented, no flag reader/read, and non-authorizing.
Kill-switch boundary remains visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, no live check, and non-authorizing.
Prerequisite gap matrix includes exactly runtime mode flag implementation and live kill-switch wiring, both unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, and non-authorizing.
Implementation risk boundary remains contract-only, blocked, and non-authorizing.
Risk register includes eight blocking/non-authorizing implementation risks.
Blocked future slices include runtime mode flag reader, live kill-switch wiring, control-path execution, and reporting sink write.

Capability and proof boundaries keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.

All side-effect truth fields preserve no module/CLI/test implementation, UI/runtime/server/listener/network/db/store/file migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/shell/PTY/browser/window/capture/control execution/reporting sink/send/deploy/trade/flag-read/kill-switch-wiring/output-file behavior.

Next recommendations are Tier0/Tier1 only, non-authorizing, and do not recommend live UI, browser/window/capture access, live runtime, customer send, deploy, trade, output files, store writes, queue/lease creation, control execution, reporting sink, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or local execution.
